chapter-04-split-01-q01.explanation: Atomic number = number of protons in nucleus.

chapter-04-split-01-q02.explanation: Mass number A = Z + N (protons + neutrons).

chapter-04-split-01-q03.explanation: Isotopes have same atomic number but different mass numbers.

chapter-04-split-01-q05.explanation: Increasing nuclear charge pulls electrons closer → radius decreases.

chapter-04-split-01-q06.explanation: Higher nuclear charge → more tightly held electrons → IE increases.

chapter-04-split-01-q07.correctOption: Across period to right and up the group
chapter-04-split-01-q07.explanation: Most EN at top-right (excluding noble gases); increases right & up.

chapter-04-split-01-q10.correctOption: Inertness due to full valence shell
chapter-04-split-01-q10.explanation: Noble gases have full valence shells → low reactivity.

chapter-04-split-01-q11.explanation: F- (9+1), O2- (8+2), Na+ (11-1) all have 10 electrons → isoelectronic.

chapter-04-split-01-q14.explanation: Metals are on left; K is metal whereas Cl is nonmetal.

chapter-04-split-01-q15.correctOption: Energy change when an atom gains an electron
chapter-04-split-01-q15.explanation: Electron affinity = energy change on gaining an electron (often negative).

chapter-04-split-01-q17.explanation: Chlorine gains one electron to form Cl- (chloride).

chapter-04-split-01-q19.explanation: Across period, radius decreases → Al (to the right) smallest among listed.

chapter-04-split-01-q20.explanation: Transition metal iron shows multiple oxidation states.

chapter-04-split-01-q21.explanation: Mg loses two electrons → Mg2+; O2- balances charge.

chapter-04-split-01-q22.explanation: Boron shows properties between metals and nonmetals → metalloid.

chapter-04-split-01-q23.explanation: A large jump indicates removal of core electron after valence removed.

chapter-04-split-01-q24.explanation: Alkali metal K loses one electron → K+.

chapter-04-split-01-q25.explanation: Sodium ions produce intense yellow flame.
